title: Persistent Connections, Pipelining, Ordering, Reuse, and Closure · Program Defense
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for persistent connections, pipelining, ordering, reuse, and closure and requires the smallest strict persistent-connection request and response state machine with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for persistent-connection request and response state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.
Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
Preserve request-response ordering across pipelined HTTP/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Persistence Defaults · changed case · Quiz Persistence Defaults
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 8180 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.

Which action demonstrates Persistence Defaults without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Persistence Defaults · changed case · Quiz Persistence Defaults.

For Persistence Defaults · changed case · Quiz Persistence Defaults, keep Persistence Defaults connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using close and discard plus specific case evidence.
